title: Timezone drift: server-local time used instead of explicit UTC/Paris anchor
root_cause: Date computations call setHours(0,0,0,0) and new Date().getFullYear() against process-local time rather than an explicit timezone anchor, so results depend on the server's TZ setting.
transversal_remediation: Anchor every business date/year computation to an explicit zone (date-fns-tz fromZonedTime / Date.UTC) as the leave-year-window helpers already do. Add a lint rule or grep gate forbidding bare setHours/getFullYear in services. Note current prod runs UTC, which masks COR-001 today but breaks the moment the app is deployed to a non-UTC host.

title: Non-atomic multi-write operations (missing $transaction / TOCTOU windows)
root_cause: Business operations that mutate multiple rows perform sequential writes (and count-then-create checks) outside any wrapping transaction, leaving partial-failure and concurrent-replay windows.
transversal_remediation: Wrap each multi-step mutation in prisma.$transaction and replace count-then-create invariant checks with atomic conditional writes (unique constraint or SELECT ... FOR UPDATE / conditional WHERE). Establish a convention: any handler issuing >1 write must use a tx client.

title: JWT access tokens survive credential change (nbf not bumped)
root_cause: Password-change paths revoke refresh tokens but never bump the per-user not-before (nbf) marker, so already-issued access tokens remain valid until natural expiry.
transversal_remediation: Call jwtNotBefore.bumpUser(userId) inside both admin reset-password and self-service change-password, in the same transaction as the password write. Add a spec asserting a pre-existing access token is rejected after each path. Amplified by the 7d effective access TTL — see cluster R.

title: Input DTO validation gaps (@MaxLength / @IsUUID missing), incl. bcrypt DoS
root_cause: Free-text and identifier DTO fields lack length bounds and strong-type validators, so unbounded strings reach bcrypt (DoS) and persistence (storage abuse).
transversal_remediation: Add @MaxLength to every free-text field and @IsUUID to every id field across DTOs; prioritise LoginDto (bcrypt DoS) for phase 1. Consider a shared base validator decorator set and a test that scans DTOs for unbounded string properties.

title: Audit-trail coverage gaps: sensitive actions unlogged or logged with the wrong action code
root_cause: Several sensitive mutations and authorization events have no audit emitter, and one event emits a misleading action code, so the audit_logs table does not faithfully reflect security-relevant activity.
transversal_remediation: Emit audit rows for user create/import, access-denied (in the guard), and document download; correct generateResetToken to a token-issued action distinct from PASSWORD_CHANGED. Add a compile-time/test check that every AuditAction enum value has at least one emitter call site.

title: Audit-log subsystem: integrity, throughput, and lifecycle weaknesses
root_cause: The hash-chained audit_logs subsystem has an out-of-transaction write ordering bug, a manual-only chain recompute with no CI guard, a global advisory lock that serialises all writes, a missing sort index, and no retention/partitioning.
transversal_remediation: Move the USER_DELETED write inside the erasure transaction; automate chain recompute as a migration post-hook or CI step with an assertion; add the (createdAt DESC, id DESC) index; evaluate batching/sequence-based chaining to relax the advisory-lock bottleneck; declare and enforce a retention/partitioning policy.

title: Unbounded list/aggregate queries (no bounded pagination ceiling)
root_cause: List and aggregate service methods default to a 1000-row limit or no limit at all, so payload and memory grow with data volume.
transversal_remediation: Introduce a shared pagination helper with a hard max page size (<=100) and apply it to every findMany that can return user/task/project/leave/snapshot collections; convert in-memory aggregates (analytics, project stats, snapshots) to groupBy/aggregate so unbounded rows never enter Node memory.

title: Per-row DB round-trips and redundant queries (N+1)
root_cause: Bulk imports and graph walks issue one or more DB queries per row/node, and analytics fires duplicate groupBy queries, multiplying latency and connection pressure.
transversal_remediation: Batch imports with createMany / pre-fetched lookup sets (resolve holidays once before the row loop); replace the dependency BFS with a single recursive CTE; memoise/deduplicate the repeated analytics groupBy; use one groupBy/aggregate for time-entry totals. Ensure all per-row reads inside a tx use the tx client.

title: Missing DB indexes on FK reverse and lookup columns
root_cause: Foreign-key reverse columns used for traversal and deletion lack covering indexes, forcing sequential scans.
transversal_remediation: Add @@index([dependsOnTaskId]) to TaskDependency and @@index([userId]) to TaskRACI (plus a migration). Audit all relations for an index on the non-primary side that is queried.

title: Referential integrity: missing/untyped FKs and nullable critical FK
root_cause: Some relations are modelled as plain String columns without a foreign key, and a security-critical FK (User.roleId) is nullable, allowing dangling references and RBAC bypass.
transversal_remediation: Convert TaskRACI.userId and UserSkill.validatedBy to real relations with FKs and an explicit onDelete policy; backfill and promote User.roleId to NOT NULL with a default/guard so no user can exist without a role.

title: Missing DB CHECK constraints (ranges, ordering, non-negative)
root_cause: Domain ranges and intra-row ordering are enforced only in application code (or not at all), leaving the database able to store out-of-range/inconsistent rows.
transversal_remediation: Add CHECK constraints via migration: progress >= 0; dayOfWeek/recurrenceDay BETWEEN 0 AND 6; weekInterval/ordinal/dayOfMonth ranges; endTime >= startTime; endDate >= startDate. Mirror the existing schema-constraint migration pattern.

title: Cross-entity/cross-project invariants not enforced on all paths
root_cause: Same-project relation invariants are checked on create but not on update, and a null-vs-null comparison lets orphan tasks bypass the check entirely.
transversal_remediation: Centralise the 'epic/milestone/dependency must share the task's project' invariant in one validator used by both create and update, and special-case projectId === null (orphan) to forbid cross-linking rather than silently allow it.

title: Transport and security hardening (headers, timing, CI secrets, info disclosure)
root_cause: Edge and infra configuration omit HSTS/redirect, trust a spoofable forwarded-proto header, use a timing-unsafe token compare, embed CI credentials in YAML, and expose an endpoint listing publicly.
transversal_remediation: Add HSTS + HTTP->HTTPS redirect in nginx and derive proto from $scheme; switch the metrics token compare to timingSafeEqual; move CI credentials to GitHub Secrets; gate or trim the root endpoint listing. These are independent quick wins.

title: Observability infrastructure absent or stubbed
root_cause: Request-ID propagation, external error tracking, durable metrics, dependency metrics, and distributed tracing are scaffolded but unwired or in-memory only, so production incidents are hard to diagnose.
transversal_remediation: Wire getRequestId() into the logger and audit payloads; implement the Sentry/error-reporter sink; export metrics to a durable store (Prometheus scrape + persistence) and add DB-pool/Redis-latency gauges; adopt OpenTelemetry for end-to-end tracing.

title: Controller spec coverage gaps
root_cause: 13 of 35 controllers have no controller-level spec, leaving their HTTP wiring, permission decorators, and DTO binding untested.
transversal_remediation: Add controller specs following the apps/api/src/tasks pattern, prioritising settings, leave-types, rbac/roles, and analytics-advanced (non-trivial permission/business logic). Each spec must include both authorized and 403 negative cases.

title: Weak / void test assertions that cannot fail
root_cause: Several tests assert tautologies, screenshot-only, swallowed-selector URL-only, or negated status codes, so a broken page or wrong status still passes.
transversal_remediation: Replace expect(true).toBeTruthy() / .not.toBe(401) / URL-only assertions with concrete positive assertions on rendered content or exact status; remove screenshot-only 'tests' or convert to visual-regression with a baseline. Add a lint rule banning expect(true) and .not.toBe(<status>).

title: E2E convention violations and coverage gaps
root_cause: Legacy E2E specs use UI login instead of the mandated API storageState pattern, contain unconditional skips, omit RBAC denial cases, and most migrations have no integration assertion.
transversal_remediation: Migrate the 5 legacy specs to the asRole()/storageState API-auth pattern; resolve or delete seed-dependent skips; add contributeur/observateur 403 cases; add integration assertions for behaviorally-significant migrations (dat015, dat021, dat025).

title: Environment/config template drift and completeness
root_cause: Several boot-relevant variables are missing from or stale in the env/compose templates (METRICS_TOKEN, JWT_ACCESS_TTL, AUDIT_HASH_KEY, deprecated ALLOWED_ORIGINS), causing insecure-by-default behavior and operator confusion.
transversal_remediation: Reconcile .env.production.example, init-env.sh and docker-compose.prod.yml with the variables the code actually reads; document METRICS_TOKEN (fail closed if unset), JWT_ACCESS_TTL, AUDIT_HASH_KEY; replace ALLOWED_ORIGINS with CORS_ORIGIN. Add a boot-time assertion + template-vs-code drift test.

title: Raw console.* in business code bypassing the central logger
root_cause: A few service methods log via console.error/console.warn instead of the NestJS Logger, bypassing Fastify log redaction and structured output.
transversal_remediation: Replace console.* with the injected Logger; add an ESLint no-console rule scoped to apps/api/src (excluding bootstrap) to prevent regression.

title: Frontend rendering and data-fetching architecture
root_cause: Every App Router page is a client component using useEffect+useState with no RSC and no TanStack Query (despite it being installed), and reference data is refetched on each interaction, inflating bundle size and request volume.
transversal_remediation: Adopt RSC for static/shell content and TanStack Query for client data with cached reference lookups; this is a larger architectural effort and should be staged after correctness/security fixes.
